title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: systemd bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for systemd is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: The systemd packages contain systemd, a system and service manager for Linux, compatible with the SysV and LSB init scripts. It provides aggressive parallelism capabilities, uses socket and D-Bus activation for starting services, offers on-demand starting of daemons, and keeps track of processes using Linux cgroups. In addition, it supports snapshotting and restoring of the system state, maintains mount and automount points, and implements an elaborate transactional dependency-based service control logic. It can also work as a drop-in replacement for sysvinit.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): systemd-cryptsetup-generator fails to successfully parse all device entries in /etc/crypttab when a unit file for a device exists, and/or a duplicate entry is present. (JIRA:RHEL-38859)  Fix parse-hwdb on RHEL 8.10 (JIRA:RHEL-130979)  user-runtime-dir@.service fails to stop if the given user doesn't exist anymore (JIRA:RHEL-132175)  [FJ8.10 Bug]: systemd: encountering ordering cycle triggers segmentation fault (JIRA:RHEL-132317)
affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 10 x86_64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for ARM 64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian
full_text:
An update for systemd is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

The systemd packages contain systemd, a system and service manager for Linux, compatible with the SysV and LSB init scripts. It provides aggressive parallelism capabilities, uses socket and D-Bus activation for starting services, offers on-demand starting of daemons, and keeps track of processes using Linux cgroups. In addition, it supports snapshotting and restoring of the system state, maintains mount and automount points, and implements an elaborate transactional dependency-based service control logic. It can also work as a drop-in replacement for sysvinit.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): systemd-cryptsetup-generator fails to successfully parse all device entries in /etc/crypttab when a unit file for a device exists, and/or a duplicate entry is present. (JIRA:RHEL-38859)  Fix parse-hwdb on RHEL 8.10 (JIRA:RHEL-130979)  user-runtime-dir@.service fails to stop if the given user doesn't exist anymore (JIRA:RHEL-132175)  [FJ8.10 Bug]: systemd: encountering ordering cycle triggers segmentation fault (JIRA:RHEL-132317)
